# Project Annotations List
> A comprehensive, categorized cheat-sheet of every Java annotation used across the password manager microservices.

*(This list was dynamically generated by scanning the entire `src/main/java` directory across all modules to ensure 100% accuracy).*

---

## 1. Spring Boot & Core Beans
These annotations tell Spring how to boot up and how to manage Java objects (`Beans`) in memory.

* `@SpringBootApplication`: Marks the main entry point of a microservice. Auto-configures everything.
* `@Component`: Marks a generic Java class to be managed by Spring.
* `@Service`: A specialized `@Component` for writing business logic (e.g., `VaultService.java`).
* `@Repository`: A specialized `@Component` that interacts with the database.
* `@Configuration`: Marks a class that contains manual setup instructions for Spring.
* `@Bean`: Used inside `@Configuration` to manually create an object for Spring to manage.
* `@Value`: Injects a property from `application.yml` directly into a variable (e.g., `@Value("${jwt.secret}")`).
* `@ConfigurationProperties`: Binds a whole block of YAML properties to a Java class.
* `@Async`: Tells Spring to run a method in a background thread without blocking the main program.

---

## 2. Spring Web (REST APIs)
These handle HTTP traffic coming from the frontend or API Gateway.

* `@RestController`: Tells Spring this class handles web requests and returns JSON by default.
* `@RequestMapping`: Defines the base URL for an entire controller (e.g., `@RequestMapping("/api/vault")`).
* `@GetMapping` / `@PostMapping` / `@PutMapping` / `@DeleteMapping`: Maps specific HTTP methods to Java functions.
* `@RequestBody`: Automatically converts the incoming JSON HTTP body into a Java object.
* `@PathVariable`: Extracts a dynamic variable from the URL (e.g., `/api/vault/{id}`).
* `@RequestParam`: Extracts a query parameter from the URL (e.g., `/api/vault?category=SOCIAL`).
* `@RequestHeader`: Extracts a specific HTTP header (like `X-User-Name`).
* `@ControllerAdvice`: A global catch-all class that intercepts errors thrown by ANY controller.
* `@ExceptionHandler`: Used inside `@ControllerAdvice` to catch a specific exception and return a custom JSON error response.
* `@ResponseStatus`: Explicitly sets the HTTP status code (e.g., `404 Not Found`).

---

## 3. Spring Cloud (Microservice Networking)
These wire the microservices together for dynamic routing and configuration.

* `@EnableDiscoveryClient`: Tells a microservice to register itself with the Eureka phonebook.
* `@EnableEurekaServer`: Turns the standard Spring Boot app into the Eureka Server registry.
* `@EnableConfigServer`: Turns the standard Spring Boot app into the Config Server.
* `@EnableFeignClients`: Turns on the Feign engine to scan for `@FeignClient` interfaces.
* `@FeignClient`: Marks an interface as an internal HTTP client (e.g., `@FeignClient(name="user-service")`).

---

## 4. Spring Data JPA (Database & Hibernate)
These annotations seamlessly translate Java objects into MySQL database tables.

### Tables & Columns
* `@Entity`: Tells Hibernate this Java class represents a database table.
* `@Table`: Allows you to customize the actual table name in SQL.
* `@Id` & `@GeneratedValue`: Denotes the Primary Key, and tells MySQL to auto-increment it.
* `@Column`: Customizes the column rules (e.g., `nullable = false`, `unique = true`).

### Relations
* `@OneToOne`, `@OneToMany`, `@ManyToOne`: Defines how tables connect to each other (Foreign Keys).
* `@JoinColumn`: Specifies the exact name of the foreign key column in the database.
* `@ElementCollection` & `@CollectionTable`: Handles lists of simple items (like a List of tags) without needing a full `@Entity`.

### Timestamps & Enums
* `@CreationTimestamp` & `@UpdateTimestamp`: Automatically writes the exact date/time when a row is inserted or updated.
* `@Enumerated`: Tells Hibernate how to save an Enum (as an Integer or a String).
* `@JdbcTypeCode`: A specialized Hibernate 6 annotation to map complex JSON columns into Java.

### Queries & Conversions
* `@Query`: Allows you to write raw SQL or JPQL when the standard methods aren't enough.
* `@Modifying`: Required above `@Query` when executing`UPDATE` or `DELETE` queries.
* `@Transactional`: Wraps a method in a database transaction—if it crashes halfway, the DB rolls back safely.
* `@Converter` & `@Convert`: Used to automatically encrypt data before it goes into the DB, and decrypt it when it comes out.

---

## 5. Spring Security
* `@EnableWebSecurity`: Turns off default Spring Security passwords and lets you define a custom `SecurityFilterChain`.
* `@AuthenticationPrincipal`: Automatically injects the currently logged-in user into a controller method.

---

## 6. AOP (Aspect-Oriented) & Scheduling
* `@Aspect`: Marks a class as an interceptor (e.g., `LoggingAspect`).
* `@Around` / `@AfterThrowing` / `@Pointcut`: Defines *when* and *where* the interceptor should trigger (e.g., "Run this timer around every VaultController method").
* `@EnableScheduling`: Turns on Spring's internal cron-job engine.
* `@Scheduled`: Runs a specific method automatically on a timer (e.g., `cron = "0 0 2 * * ?"` to clear trash at 2 AM).

---

## 7. Lombok (Code Reduction)
These annotations are compiled away by Maven and generate standard Java code invisibly.

* `@Data`: Generates Getters, Setters, `toString()`, and `equals()` for an entire class.
* `@Builder`: Allows object creation using the fluent builder pattern (`obj.builder().name("x").build()`).
* `@NoArgsConstructor` / `@AllArgsConstructor`: Generates empty or full constructors.
* `@RequiredArgsConstructor`: Generates a constructor ONLY for fields marked `final` (perfect for Dependency Injection).
* `@Slf4j`: Automatically creates a `log` variable for printing to the console without boilerplate.
* `@NonNull`: Tells Lombok to throw a NullPointerException if a value is null.

---

## 8. Validation (Jakarta / Hibernate)
These ensure data coming from the frontend is clean before it hits your service logic.

* `@Valid`: Triggers the validation checks on an incoming nested object.
* `@NotNull`: Ensure a field is not null.
* `@NotBlank`: Ensures a text string isn't null AND isn't just empty spaces.
* `@Email`: Runs a regex check to ensure the string looks like an email.
* `@Size` / `@Min` / `@Max`: Limits the length of strings or values of integers.
* `@AssertTrue`: Validates that a boolean field is true (often used for "I agree to terms" checkboxes).

---

## 9. Jackson (JSON Parsing) & Swagger (API Docs)
* `@JsonProperty`: Customizes the name of a variable when it is converted to JSON.
* `@JsonInclude`: Tells Jackson to ignore sending null fields to the frontend to save bandwidth.
* `@Tag` / `@Operation` / `@Schema`: Used by SpringDoc OpenAPI to auto-generate beautiful API documentation titles and descriptions in Swagger UI.
